subject: Biology
title: Universal Vaccines: One Shot for All
description: One-minute skill: Universal Vaccines: One Shot for All.

hook.text: Imagine one flu shot that protects against every strain forever. Scientists are getting closer.

buildup.text: Current flu vaccines target parts of the virus that mutate. Universal vaccines target stable regions.

discovery.text: The stem of the influenza surface protein barely changes. Antibodies against it could neutralize all strains.

twist.text: A universal coronavirus vaccine could prevent the next pandemic before it even starts.

climax.text: Several candidates are in clinical trials. Success would transform global health forever.

punchline.text: One shot. Every strain. The ultimate vaccine.

quiz.question: What makes a universal vaccine different from current flu vaccines?
options: It targets stable viral regions that rarely mutate
It uses a stronger dose of the same vaccine
It combines all existing vaccines into one
